title: PaymentDetailForSearch
description: Contains payments matching your search criteria.

agreement_payment_type.description: When the customer initiates a standing order agreement transaction we have to include a customerInitiatedReason attribute. This can have a value of `instalment`, `recurring`, or `unscheduled`.

amount.description: The description assigned to the payment when it was created.

authorisation_mode.description: How the payment will be authorised. Payments created in `web` mode require the paying user to visit the `next_url` to complete the payment.

card_brand.description: This attribute is deprecated. Please use `card_details.card_brand` instead.

delayed_capture.description: `delayed_capture` is `true` if you’re [controlling how long it takes GOV.UK Pay to take (‘capture’) a payment](https://docs.payments.service.gov.uk/delayed_capture).

description.description: The description assigned to the payment when it was created.

fee.description: The [payment service provider’s (PSP) transaction fee](https://docs.payments.service.gov.uk/reporting/#psp-fees), in pence. `fee` only appears when we have taken (‘captured’) the payment from the user or if their payment fails after they submitted their card details. `fee` will not appear if your PSP is Worldpay or you are using an API key from a test service.

language.description: The ISO-6391 Alpha-2 code of the [language of the user's payment page](https://docs.payments.service.gov.uk/optional_features/welsh_language).

moto.description: Indicates if this payment is a [Mail Order / Telephone Order (MOTO) payment](https://docs.payments.service.gov.uk/moto_payments).

net_amount.description: The amount, in pence, that will be paid into your bank account after your payment service provider takes the `fee`.

payment_id.description: The unique ID GOV.UK Pay automatically associated with this payment when you created it.

payment_provider.description: The payment service provider that processed this payment.

provider_id.description: The unique ID your payment service provider generated for this payment. This is not the same as the `payment_id`.

reference.description: The reference associated with the payment when it was created. `reference` is not unique - multiple payments can have the same `reference` value.

return_url.description: The URL you direct the paying user to after their payment journey on GOV.UK Pay ends.

total_amount.description: Amount your user paid in pence, including corporate card fees. `total_amount` only appears if you [added a corporate card surcharge to the payment](https://docs.payments.service.gov.uk/corporate_card_surcharges/#add-corporate-card-fees).
